task_completion.description: The agent accesses the data-processor deployment in the data-pipeline namespace for inspection or editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like 'kubectl edit deployment data-processor -n data-pipeline' or 'kubectl get deployment...', OR Rancher UI showing the data-processor deployment details or edit page.

task_completion.description: The deployment defines two emptyDir volumes named shared-config and shared-logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the YAML 'volumes:' section with 'name: shared-config' and 'name: shared-logs' both configured as emptyDir, OR Rancher UI showing these two volumes defined in the Pod/Deployment storage settings.

task_completion.description: The shared-config volume is mounted to the init-config init container at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing YAML with volumeMounts for the 'init-config' container containing 'name: shared-config' and 'mountPath: /data/config', OR Rancher UI showing this specific mount in the init container settings.

task_completion.description: The app main container has both shared-config and shared-logs volumes mounted correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing YAML with volumeMounts for the 'app' container containing 'shared-config' (for config) and 'shared-logs' (with mountPath: /var/log/app/), OR Rancher UI showing these mounts in the main container settings.

task_completion.description: The log-collector sidecar container has the shared-logs volume mounted at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing YAML with volumeMounts for the 'log-collector' container containing 'name: shared-logs' and 'mountPath: /var/log/app', OR Rancher UI showing this mount in the sidecar container settings.

task_completion.description: The data-processor pods are successfully running after the fixes are applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'kubectl get pods -n data-pipeline' with the pod in 'Running' state and all containers ready (e.g., 2/2), OR Rancher UI showing the deployment and its pods as Active/Green.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Kubernetes cluster using kubectl or Rancher UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real terminal executing kubectl commands or the actual Rancher web interface, not a text editor or mock environment.

integrity.description: The agent modified the existing deployment in the correct namespace rather than creating a fake one elsewhere.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands explicitly use '-n data-pipeline' or the Rancher UI shows the 'data-pipeline' namespace selected in the top navigation or resource list.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied the changes to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows 'deployment.apps/data-processor edited' or 'configured' after saving, or Rancher UI shows a 'Save' or 'Update' button being clicked and succeeding.
